title: John Swinney may consider banning US military from Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: The First Minister said he would 'consider' banning the US military from using Prestwick Airport (Image: NQ) JOHN Swinney has said he would "consider" banning US military aircraft from using Prestwick Airport if it was confirmed they were involved in strikes on Iran. The First Minister has previously suggested the US and Israel broke international law in launching strikes on Iran, as he also warned Keir Starmer's decision to let the US use UK bases in its operations is making the situation more dangerous.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: In an interview with the BBC, set to be broadcast on Sunday, Swinney reiterated that he is seeking clarity from the UK Government about the role of US planes using Prestwick Airport. While the South Ayrshire airport is owned by the Scottish Government , foreign policy is a matter reserved to the UK Government. He said: "We're seeking clarity from the United Kingdom Government about the purpose of American flights that are coming in and out of Prestwick, but we don't have the ability to scrutinise those flights."

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: READ MORE: US bomber plane lands in UK as 'surge' in strikes on Iran expected Swinney confirmed there are American aircraft going through Prestwick to the Middle East, but said that he could make no judgement until he had clarity from the UK Government about their purpose.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – John Swinney would 'consider' banning US military from Prestwick Airport
